feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: License model of IAG
entry.content: can you please confirm if provisioning BTP/IAS/IPS via IAG access requests will incur Integration license of IAG or the Standard one.

entry.title: IAG: how to select users to be sync
entry.content:
Hi all

Is there a way to control the number of users to be synced into IAG, from SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public edition?

Currently the job does sync all users deployed in the application.

Thank you

entry.title: GRC prod system provisioning to Prod and non prod systems
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

Has anyone considered and managed / failed to setup GRC Prod environment to provision to all connected Prod and non prod systems through IAG/IAS prod tenants

Short story - GRC ARM request loaded with business role and IAG/IAS element would provision to both prod and non prod cloud apps.

entry.title: Generative AI Powered IAM Business Role Augmentation (GAIRA)
entry.content:
Generative AI Powered IAM Business Role Augmentation (GAIRA) is a transformative concept for modern enterprises, shifting Identity and Access Management (IAM) from manual, reactive processes to a proactive, intelligent, and efficient system.

Limitations of traditional IAM include:

- Manual Provisioning: Managing user accounts and privileges can be time-consuming and error-prone, particularly in large organizations.
- Inflexible Security: Rule-based systems often lack the context needed to detect subtle, behavioral-based anomalies indicative of modern threats, such as insider risks.
- Compliance Burden: The manual nature of access reviews and audit trail generation drains resources and struggles to keep pace with regulatory changes.
- Subpar User Experience: Slow access request workflows can frustrate users and hinder productivity.

GAIRA addresses these challenges by integrating generative AI into the four pillars of IAM:

1. Adaptive Authentication
- Risk-Based Access: GAIRA enables continuous, risk-based authentication by analyzing user behavior, location, and device in real-time. For instance, a login attempt from an unusual location could trigger additional verification steps.
- Frictionless Security: This dynamic security approach balances convenience and trust, enhancing security without burdening trusted users.

2. Intelligent Authorization
- Automated Role Management: Generative AI analyzes historical access data and user roles to recommend optimal role assignments, significantly reducing manual effort and errors.
- Contextual Access Recommendations: For access requests needing approval, GAIRA provides context-rich insights based on peer-group comparisons and historical outcomes, facilitating faster, informed decisions.

3. Streamlined Administration
- Lifecycle Automation: GAIRA automates the entire user lifecycle, from onboarding to offboarding, dynamically assigning and removing access rights based on predefined policies and roles.
- Enhanced User Experience: Users can interact with the IAM system using natural language via conversational AI interfaces, improving productivity and reducing the burden on IT help desks.

entry.title: IAG Access Request: creating a new user in ECC backend for an existing IAS user
entry.content:
Dear all,

As an IAG user I create a new request in AR, choosing as Access Type "Application" and then I choose the ECC onprem application we have integrated with IAG. My user doesn't exist in ECC yet. Then after the request has been approved, the Provisioning job fails because it tries to create the ECC user as "<First Name in IAS><space><Last Name in IAS> which of course fails. Is there any way to tell IAG to create the backend user with other name, for example the Display Name in IAG, or other value?

entry.title: IAS Tenant
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

As far as I know, it was previously recommended to use the Test IAS tenant only for testing purposes (i.e. Sandbox), and to use the Productive IAS tenant for productive cloud applications as well as for development and test environments.

Previous recommendation:

  • IAS Test → Sandbox environments
  • IAS Prod → PROD, DEV, and QAS

My question to the community:

What is the current recommended approach?

Should this setup still be followed:

    • IAS Test → Sandbox environments (only testing)
    • IAS Prod → PROD, DEV, and QAS ( SAC PROD + SAC QAS)

Or does SAP now recommend a different model, for example:

  • IAS Test → for development and test environments
  • IAS Prod → only for PROD

I would like to make this decision for our landscape (e.g. SAC QAS & PROD) and would appreciate an up-to-date assessment of which option is currently considered Best Practice.

Thank you in advance

Best Regards

entry.title: SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance License Counting Logic
entry.content:
For Expired Users in SAP ERP and SAP S/4HANA, they will not be counted for SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance License, right?

When a SAP S/4HANA User is deemed to be expired, will IAG automatically stop monitoring that user and will the license consumption be reduced by 1? Thanks.

entry.title: Autopath in IAG is not working for PAM requests
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

We have activated the rulesets in SAP IAG so when a user creates an access requests and chooses 'AUTO_APPROVAL' as request reason, the autopath workflow (no approvers required) is used. 

This works fine for normal access requests, but if we request a PAM user, this message appears:

"PAM Assignment request cannot be auto approved. Please contact your IAG Administrator to correct the access request workflow configuration"

We have also tried with other different rule, which is based on the request type. In case it is 'PAM', the autopath is used. The behaviour is just like the one we have with the first case, same message. 

Is there any way in SAP IAG to activate auto-approvals for Privileged Accesses?

Thank you

Francisco

entry.title: Error: While connecting from SAP BTP Integration Suite to SAP S4 Cloud Odata
entry.content:
Hello Team,

 

I'm getting below error while connecting from SAP BTP Integration Suite Cloud to SAP S4 Cloud Odata service.

"com.sap.gateway.core.ip.component.odata.exception.OsciException: HTTP Request failed with error : <HOST>: No address associated with hostname, cause: java.net.UnknownHostException: <HOST>: No address associated with hostname"

 

 

 

Thanks and Regards,

Rajesh PS

entry.title: FF SAP Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello, 

I would like to know if it is possible to create and use Firefighter IDs in SAP Public Cloud, similar to how we do in the on-premise version. Additionally, is SAP Identity Access Governance (IAG) required for this functionality?
If this is not possible, what replaces or substitutes this concept in the SAP Public Cloud environment?

Thank you

entry.title: SAP IAS Admin Console
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

What would be the best way to secure the SAP IAS Administration Console?

Is it a good idea to restrict access to a specific IP range (e.g. only the company network)? I’m concerned about locking myself out in case the IP address changes.

Or would it be better to implement 2FA for an admin group containing all admins? In that case, there’s also the risk of locking myself out.

I would appreciate your experiences and tips!

Many Thanks 

Best Regards

entry.title: SAP IAG Access Request Status audit logs
entry.content:
Seeking advisory guidance on SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance (8010452)

The IAG customer would like to generate a report that shows access requests approved by an individual user or their team members.

In addition, they would like to view audit logs within the Access Request Status app. Is it possible to enable this?

Currently, the available IAG reports are administrative reports that display all access requests and therefore cannot be shared with business users.

entry.title: IAS Login Name dependencies
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Quick question about the Login Name field in IAS:

Does it matter what I enter here? I often see an email address, but could I also use an employee ID or a short name?

Are there SAP applications or scenarios that require the "Login Name" to be a specific attribute (e.g. Email), or is the field completely flexible?

I want to avoid SSO issues with specific apps if the value is wrong. Does anyone have experience with dependencies for this field?

Many Thanks

Best Regards

entry.title: IAG: Assign Mitigation Control at User level
entry.content: Is Mitigation control assignment possible at User level. if so through which app is the same possible

entry.title: Does Sap Cloud Identity Services (CIS) - IAS/IPS support position based security?
entry.content:
I am interested to know if the new age SAP Cloud Identity solutions suppor the position based security for S/4HANA (HCM on S/4HANA)?
I could not find any mention of this anywhere on sap help documentations.

entry.title: IAG–S/4HANA User ID Mapping Requirement
entry.content:
Hi

I have requirement regarding an integration involving SAP Identity Access Governance (IAG), SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS/IPS), and our on-premise SAP S/4HANA system.

We are currently provisioning users from Microsoft Entra ID into SAP Cloud Identity Services, where users will create and assigned P-User IDs. Once the User IDs created in CIS, We are maintaining “Login Name” as our required naming convention (XXYYZZNN) for S/4 HANA system.

As per SAP Recommendation we are creating user IDs as P user IDs in BTP-IAG Subaccount. However, our S/4HANA system requires user IDs to follow a specific internal naming convention (XXYYZZNN). We need guidance and confirmation on the recommended approach to ensure that:

1.SAP IAG provisions users into S/4HANA with our required naming convention (XXYYZZNN) instead of the IAS P-User ID.

2. Attribute mapping and transformation rules between IAS, IPS, IAG, and S/4HANA. We would appreciate assistance advising on the correct configuration steps for attribute mapping and user ID transformation within IAG.

entry.title: Value Help Restrictions in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Do They Filter the F4 Help?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently analysing the behaviour of Value Help restrictions in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and would like to clarify whether my understanding of the concept is correct.

My question is not limited to a specific field, but concerns the general design of Value Help restrictions in business roles.

Background

In the Maintain Business Roles app it is possible to define Value Help restrictions for certain fields within an access category.

The expectation from a usability and security perspective would be that these restrictions limit the values displayed in the F4 value help, so that users only see entries they are allowed to use.

However, in my testing the behaviour appears different.

Example Scenario (Sales Order)

To illustrate the behaviour, I tested this in the sales order creation process.

Applications tested:

  • VA01 – Create Sales Order

  • F3893 –  (Fiori)

Goal:

Restrict the Sales Organization (VKORG) so that users can only work with a limited set of values.

Configuration steps:

  1. Open Maintain Business Roles.

  2. Edit the business role.

  3. Navigate to Restrictions.

  4. In the Sales Area section, maintain Value Help restrictions for Sales Organization.

  5. Example configuration:

    • Allow only a subset of Sales Organizations (e.g. 1710, 1720)

    • Alternatively test with no entries maintained.

Observed Behaviour

When the user creates a sales order and presses F4 on the Sales Organization field:

  • The value help always shows all Sales Organizations in the system.

  • The maintained value help restrictions are not reflected in the F4 help.

This happens in both:

  • VA01 (GUI)

  • Fiori app F3893

However, when the user selects a Sales Organization that should not be allowed and tries to continue or save the sales order, the system raises an authorization error, for example:

“You are not authorized for Sales Organization XYZ”

This indicates that the restriction is technically evaluated, but only during document processing, not during the value help selection.

 

Conceptual Question

My main question is about the general design of Value Help restrictions in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Should Value Help restrictions:

Option A

Filter the F4 value help, so that users only see values they are authorized for.

or

Option B

Allow users to see all values in the value help, but enforce the restriction only when the document is processed or saved.

Additional Question

Independently of the Sales Organization example:

Is there any mechanism in S/4HANA Public Cloud that allows system-wide hiding of values in value help based on authorizations or restrictions?

In other words:

Is it generally possible that unauthorized values are not visible at all in F4 helps, or is the standard design that they remain visible and are only blocked during processing?

Any insights from others working with authorizations and business roles in S/4HANA Public Cloud would be very helpful.

Thank you.

David

entry.title: IAG-Access Analysis in Public cloud S/4 HANA at Fiori application level
entry.content:
Hello, In S/4 public cloud 2602, we have feasibility restrict business roles at application level. in IAG Do we have the feasibility to conduct access analysis at FIORO application level or we can do at business catalog level, field level permissions etc?
I could not find any information on this. Any information on this appreciated.

Thank you

Mukesh

entry.title: Restrictions by company code and Plant in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
The client needs to restrict access at the company code and Plant level based on the end user's line of business to ensure they only have visibility in their current location within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

The client has 11 Plants and 2 company codes.

Let's assume the end user belongs to the Purchasing business line and is assigned to the Mexico City plant, which represents 1010 in SAP, while the company code is 1000.
The following standard roles are those used by the end user to run Fiori applications in the purchasing area.

Roles estándar

BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST

BR_CASH_MANAGER

BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT

BR_EMPLOYEE

BR_INVENTORY_MANAGER

BR_MATL_PLNR_EXT_PROC

BR_RECEIVING_SPECIALIST

BR_AA_ACCOUNTANT

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT_MX

BR_BPC_EXPERT

BR_PURCHASER_RFM

After that, I entered an app called “Maintain Business Roles” (F1492)

Roles in blue generated a copy of the standard because they can be restricted at the plant level (WERKS) and the company code level (BUKRS).

Roles Copia 1

Restriccion

BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST

 

BR_CASH_MANAGER_CDMX_1010

2000

BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_EMPLOYEE

 

BR_INVENTORY_MANAGER_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_MATL_PLNR_EXT_PROC_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_RECEIVING_SPECIALIST_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_AA_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010

1010, 1000

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT_MX_CDMX_1010

2000

BR_BPC_EXPERT_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

BR_PURCHASER_RFM_CDMX_1010

10201030-2000

Taking as an example one of the 10 roles, BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT, which generates 2 copies, the standard role has no restrictions, but the 2 copies as BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010 and BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010_V2 do have restrictions.

I clicked on edit and for the BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010 role I started with the restrictions.

Then click on “Maintain Restrictions”

In the “Access Categories” section, select “Restricted” for Write, Read, Value Help, and in the Assigned Restriction Types section, click on “general” where you will find the Company code and Plant fields.

For the company field, it is necessary that the end user only see the information for company code 1000, so only place the check mark for 2000 as a restriction.

In the plant field, select all plants except 1010, which represents Mexico City, because I want the user to only have access to that plant and be restricted from the others: 1020, 1030, 1040, 1050, 1060, etc.

Finally, I clicked the "Save" button to save the changes.

I repeated the steps with the rest of the roles, creating a copy respectively, in which I only restricted the company code and the plant

Roles estándar

Roles Copia 1

BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST

BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST

BR_CASH_MANAGER

BR_CASH_MANAGER_CDMX_1010

BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT

BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010

BR_EMPLOYEE

BR_EMPLOYEE

BR_INVENTORY_MANAGER

BR_INVENTORY_MANAGER_CDMX_1010

BR_MATL_PLNR_EXT_PROC

BR_MATL_PLNR_EXT_PROC_CDMX_1010

BR_RECEIVING_SPECIALIST

BR_RECEIVING_SPECIALIST_CDMX_1010

BR_AA_ACCOUNTANT

BR_AA_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT_CDMX_1010

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT_MX

BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT_MX_CDMX_1010

BR_BPC_EXPERT

BR_BPC_EXPERT_CDMX_1010

BR_PURCHASER_RFM

BR_PURCHASER_RFM_CDMX_1010

However, when logging into the Manage Purchase orders app F0842A

I still have visibility with the other plants and I only want it to have access to the 1010 in CDMX, could you guide me on the correct restriction process? Please

entry.title: Cloud ALM User
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am working on SAP Cloud ALM setup and I am not sure what the best way is to onboard users to SAP Cloud ALM.

My setup:

- Corporate IDP
- IAS as Proxy
- Users are provisioned from the corporate IdP into IAS

Now I am unsure what the best method is for Cloud ALM user onboarding.

As far as I understand, there are these options:
1. CALM User Management App (manual user creation in Cloud ALM)
2. BTP Role Collections (central assignment via BTP Cockpit)
3. IPS group based provisioning

My questions:

What is the best practice in real projects?
What is most commonly used in production?
Are there any known issues or limitations with these approaches?

Thanks for your help!!!

Best Regards
